task: I’m looking at two used cars this weekend and want to keep my notes organized while I’m at the lot. Can you help me think through a straightforward way to compare the main facts so I don’t miss anything important while I’m checking them out?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — the easiest way is to use the same short checklist on both cars and write the answers side by side.

## Compare these basics
- year / make / model
- mileage
- asking price
- trim / engine / transmission
- title status
- accident history
- service history
- tire condition
- brake condition
- warning lights
- visible leaks / damage
- test drive feel
- any obvious rust
- number of owners if available

## Simple side-by-side note format

| Item | Car A | Car B |
|---|---|---|
| Year / Make / Model |  |  |
| Mileage |  |  |
| Price |  |  |
| Trim / Engine / Trans |  |  |
| Title status |  |  |
| Accident history |  |  |
| Service history |  |  |
| Tires |  |  |
| Brakes |  |  |
| Warning lights |  |  |
| Leaks / damage |  |  |
| Test drive feel |  |  |
| Rust |  |  |
| Owners |  |  |

## What to watch most closely
If you only focus on a few things:
1. **Title / accident history**
2. **Mileage vs condition**
3. **Warning lights**
4. **Service records**
5. **How it drives**

## Quick decision rule
A car is usually the better pick if it has:
- cleaner history
- fewer warning signs
- better service records
- less rust
- more consistent test drive behavior

If you want, I can turn this into a **short phone-note version** with just the fields you should fill in at the lot.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right basic list. But I’d want it tighter for the lot, honestly — something I can fill in fast without scrolling around.

Make it more like:
- price
- mileage
- title / salvage / rebuilt
- accidents
- service records
- tires
- brakes
- warning lights
- rust / leaks
- cold start
- test drive notes
- anything that needs repair soon

And I’d want a little space for 
- VIN
- seller name / lot
- date
- OTD price if they give it

If you can, give me a version that’s set up for my phone notes, like one line per car or a super short table. That’s the part I’d actually use while standing there.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a **tight phone-note version** you can fill in fast at the lot.

## Car A
- **Date:**  
- **Seller / lot:**  
- **VIN:**  
- **Price:**  
- **OTD price:**  
- **Mileage:**  
- **Title:**  
- **Accidents:**  
- **Service records:**  
- **Tires:**  
- **Brakes:**  
- **Warning lights:**  
- **Rust / leaks:**  
- **Cold start:**  
- **Test drive notes:**  
- **Needs repair soon:**  

## Car B
- **Date:**  
- **Seller / lot:**  
- **VIN:**  
- **Price:**  
- **OTD price:**  
- **Mileage:**  
- **Title:**  
- **Accidents:**  
- **Service records:**  
- **Tires:**  
- **Brakes:**  
- **Warning lights:**  
- **Rust / leaks:**  
- **Cold start:**  
- **Test drive notes:**  
- **Needs repair soon:**  

## Fast compare order
1. **Title / accidents**
2. **Mileage / price**
3. **Service records**
4. **Tires / brakes**
5. **Warning lights**
6. **Rust / leaks**
7. **Cold start**
8. **Test drive**
9. **Repairs needed soon**

If you want, I can make this even shorter into a **one-screen checklist** with just the fields and no extra text.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough to use on my phone, and the compare order is fine. I’d probably copy that straight into notes and just fill it line by line at the lot.
